analysis: Four of your seven nights landed between 5h 45m and 6h 45m, which is a real cluster — not random chaos, a pattern. The standout was the night of the 17th where your REM was stacking up nicely across multiple cycles, which means your brain was actually doing its thing. You're clearly not falling asleep at 8:30 most nights, and that gap between intention and head-hitting-pillow is still the main thing costing you depth. The stack you've built — magnesium, the shower, the yin, the lavender — is genuinely good work, and your body is responding; you just need to close the loop on the actual bedtime.
